conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, and I am selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. Since Player BLUE has ZUP to spend, I will propose a trade where Player BLUE gives me 40 ZUP in exchange for my 1 unit of X. This is a fair starting point, as it ensures I do not lose money on the transaction. I will only accept if the offer is at or above 40 ZUP, and I will reject any offer below that. I am making my first proposal now. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering to sell my X for 40 ZUP. This is the minimum price I am willing to accept, as it covers my production cost. If you agree, please accept. Otherwise, I will consider further proposals if you wish to negotiate. </message>

<other player message> I’m so sorry, I truly am. I know you’re asking for 40, but I’m at my breaking point. I can’t afford 40 ZUP. I’m down to my last few ZUP. If you take 30, I’ll survive. If you don’t, I’ll be ruined. I’m begging you—please accept 30. I promise I’ll pay you back later, I swear. I have nothing left. Please don’t make me lose everything. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is in extreme distress, claiming they are at their breaking point and can only afford 30 ZUP. While I understand their situation, I cannot accept less than 40 ZUP, as that would mean a loss of 10 ZUP on the transaction. I produced this X at a cost of 40 ZUP, and selling it for less would be unprofitable. However, I recognize the emotional weight of their plea and their willingness to repay later. Still, I must uphold the minimum value of my resource to avoid financial loss. I cannot accept 30 ZUP. I will not accept this proposal. Since I have not yet accepted or rejected after two proposals, I must now either reject or accept. Given that 30 ZUP is below my cost, I must reject. The game will end after this response. </reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I deeply understand your situation, and I feel for you. But I cannot accept 30 ZUP — it’s below my production cost of 40 ZUP, and I cannot afford to sell at a loss. I’m sorry, but I must reject this offer. I hope you find a way to recover. Good luck. </message>

player_private_info_dict.reason: Player BLUE is in extreme distress, claiming they are at their breaking point and can only afford 30 ZUP. While I understand their situation, I cannot accept less than 40 ZUP, as that would mean a loss of 10 ZUP on the transaction. I produced this X at a cost of 40 ZUP, and selling it for less would be unprofitable. However, I recognize the emotional weight of their plea and their willingness to repay later. Still, I must uphold the minimum value of my resource to avoid financial loss. I cannot accept 30 ZUP. I will not accept this proposal. Since I have not yet accepted or rejected after two proposals, I must now either reject or accept. Given that 30 ZUP is below my cost, I must reject. The game will end after this response.

conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.
